name: CIS Ubuntu Linux 22.04 LTS Benchmark
description: Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmark for Ubuntu Linux 22.04 LTS.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of cramfs filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The cramfs filesystem type is a compressed read-only Linux filesystem.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of freevxfs filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The freevxfs filesystem is a VxFS filesystem variant used on HP-UX.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of jffs2 filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The jffs2 filesystem is a log-structured filesystem for flash memory.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of hfs filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The hfs filesystem is used on older Macintosh computers.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of hfsplus filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The hfsplus filesystem is used on MacOS computers.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of squashfs filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The squashfs filesystem type is a compressed read-only filesystem commonly used for live systems.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure mounting of udf filesystems is disabled
controls.description: The udf filesystem is a universal disk format for optical storage.
controls.impact: Unneeded filesystem support increases the attack surface.

controls.control_name: Ensure /tmp is configured on a separate partition
controls.description: The /tmp directory is a world-writable directory used for temporary storage.
controls.remediation: Configure /etc/fstab to mount /tmp as a separate partition.
controls.remediation_command: echo 'tmpfs /tmp tmpfs defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime 0 0' >> /etc/fstab && mount -o remount /tmp || true
controls.impact: Prevents temporary files from exhausting root storage.

controls.control_name: Ensure X Window System is not installed
controls.description: The X Window System provides graphical desktop environments on Linux.
controls.impact: Decreases package footprint and potential local vulnerabilities.

controls.control_name: Ensure Avahi Daemon is not enabled
controls.description: Avahi is a zero-configuration service discovery daemon.
controls.check_command: systemctl is-enabled avahi-daemon 2>/dev/null || echo "disabled or not installed"
controls.remediation_command: systemctl stop avahi-daemon && systemctl disable avahi-daemon || true
controls.impact: Reduces network exposure via UDP 5353.

controls.control_name: Ensure CUPS is not enabled
controls.description: CUPS (Common Unix Printing System) manages printing services.
controls.check_command: systemctl is-enabled cups 2>/dev/null || echo "disabled or not installed"
controls.remediation_command: systemctl stop cups && systemctl disable cups || true
controls.impact: Reduces network surface on TCP/UDP 631.

controls.control_name: Ensure DHCP Server is not enabled
controls.description: Enabling a DHCP server allows dynamic network IP allocations.
controls.check_command: systemctl is-enabled isc-dhcp-server 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-enabled kea-dhcp4 2>/dev/null || echo "disabled or not installed"
controls.remediation_command: systemctl stop isc-dhcp-server && systemctl disable isc-dhcp-server || true
controls.impact: Prevents unauthorized DHCP configuration of client devices.

controls.control_name: Ensure HTTP server is not enabled
controls.description: An HTTP web server hosts websites (e.g. apache2, nginx).
controls.check_command: systemctl is-enabled apache2 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-enabled nginx 2>/dev/null || echo "disabled or not installed"
controls.remediation_command: systemctl stop apache2 && systemctl disable apache2 && systemctl stop nginx && systemctl disable nginx || true
controls.impact: Reduces unauthorized HTTP exposure.

controls.control_name: Ensure IP forwarding is disabled
controls.description: IP forwarding allows a system to forward packets to other subnets.
controls.impact: Prevents the system from operating as a router between networks.

controls.control_name: Ensure auditd is installed
controls.description: The audit daemon (auditd) logs key security events on the host.
controls.check_command: dpkg -s auditd 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Status: install ok installed" | awk '{print $4}' || echo "not installed"
controls.impact: Provides detailed logging of file access, processes, and network connections.

controls.control_name: Ensure auditd service is enabled
controls.description: Verify auditd is running and configured to start at boot.
controls.remediation_command: systemctl start auditd && systemctl enable auditd
controls.impact: Maintains persistent logging across reboot events.

controls.control_name: Ensure SSH Protocol is set to 2
controls.description: SSH Protocol 1 has known cryptographic weaknesses.
controls.impact: Enforces modern, secure cryptographic handshakes.

controls.control_name: Ensure SSH PermitRootLogin is disabled
controls.description: Allowing root SSH login permits brute force attempts directly on admin accounts.
controls.remediation_command: sed -i 's/^#\?PermitRootLogin.*/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && systemctl reload sshd || true
controls.impact: Enforces non-repudiation by requiring users to login as themselves first.

controls.control_name: Ensure SSH PermitEmptyPasswords is disabled
controls.description: Permitting empty passwords allows login without credentials.
controls.remediation_command: sed -i 's/^#\?PermitEmptyPasswords.*/PermitEmptyPasswords no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && systemctl reload sshd || true
controls.impact: Ensures all remote logins are properly authenticated.

controls.control_name: Ensure password expiration max days is set to 90 or less
controls.description: Limiting maximum password lifespan reduces brute-force vulnerability exposure.
controls.impact: Enforces periodic security credential changes.
